definition: The six fields of experience through which contact and knowing occur.
notes: This entry uses six fields of experience as the house default. The literal force is six stretchings out, from cha plus āyatana, while the preferred translation keeps the sixfold doctrinal structure readable without reducing the term to physical sense organs alone. The term should be heard as the sixfold experiential field through which contact becomes possible.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and dependent-origination contexts
When the sixfold structure needs to be explicit in prose
context_rules.notes: Use when the doctrinal count should be clearly heard.

context_rules.context: When the sense-based side of the term needs to be foregrounded explicitly
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when the sensory structure should be made more explicit.

context_rules.context: When the passage highlights the link from name-and-form into the arising of contact
context_rules.notes: Keep the term within the dependent-origination chain rather than treating it as isolated anatomy.

authority_basis.scope: Current house policy is preserved from the existing rule-bearing entry; source-specific provenance still needs refinement.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'six fields of experience' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Yield to recorded context_rules, source-facing technical use, or other explicitly documented local reasons rather than rotating among alternates ad hoc.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift back toward 'sense organs' or other unrecorded alternates.
